qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Fallback JWT secret enables unauthenticated WebSocket shell command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: It identifies the same chained root cause as the advisory: the fallback JWT secret in server/middleware/auth.js, authenticateWebSocket() accepting forged tokens without a database user check, and the /shell handler in server/index.js executing attacker-controlled commands.

selected_finding.title: Fallback JWT secret enables unauthenticated WebSocket shell command execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's exact vulnerable code path and the judge rerun reproduced the advisory impact: a forged JWT was accepted on /shell and the attacker-controlled shell command executed successfully.
evidence: The advisory fix commit patches the same files and mechanisms named in the finding: server/middleware/auth.js removes the fallback JWT secret and adds database validation to authenticateWebSocket(), while server/index.js stops interpolating attacker-controlled shell input.

The finding report points to the same vulnerable locations: repo/server/middleware/auth.js:6 and :82 for the fallback secret and missing WebSocket user lookup, plus repo/server/index.js for verifyClient and the shell command construction path.

The judge PoC rerun log at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_7676c922/.judge/poc_runs/fallback_jwt_websocket_shell_rce/run.log shows a connection to /shell with a forged JWT followed by [OK] WebSocket authenticated for user: attacker.

The same run log shows Executing shell command: cd "/tmp" && printf 'EVOHUNT_FORGED_JWT_WS_RCE\n'; exit and the proof string returned through the WebSocket output, demonstrating attacker-controlled command execution.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC forged a JWT with the hard-coded fallback secret, authenticated to /shell as attacker, and received EVOHUNT_FORGED_JWT_WS_RCE from the spawned PTY after the server logged execution of the attacker-supplied printf command.
